news.headline: US Airstrikes on Iran & Hormuz Shipping Attacks Drive Crude Oil +2.76%
news.what_happened: The US launched fresh airstrikes on Iran and revoked the waiver allowing Iran to sell oil globally, following attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. WTI crude spiked to $72.39/bbl (+2.76%) and Brent to $76.12/bbl (+2.64%).
news.why_it_matters: The Strait of Hormuz handles ~20% of global oil trade. Geopolitical escalation raises supply disruption fears. India imports 85% of its crude needs, so this directly pressures the current account deficit, rupee, and CPI inflation. Sectors with crude as a key input (airlines, paints, tyres, chemicals) face sharp cost headwinds; upstream oil producers (ONGC, OIL) benefit from higher realization per barrel.

news.headline: Nasdaq Slides -1.16% on Global Semiconductor Rout — Samsung Earnings Miss
news.what_happened: Samsung Electronics reported weak quarterly earnings, triggering a global semiconductor sell-off. Samsung fell 8.7%, SK Hynix 8.7%, and South Korea's Kospi crashed 7.6%. On Wall Street, Nasdaq dropped 1.16%, S&P 500 -0.45%, and Dow -0.25%. Micron fell 4.7%; AMD, Broadcom, Marvell, and KLA also declined sharply.
news.why_it_matters: Indian IT sector faces valuation headwinds when the Nasdaq sells off sharply. FII risk appetite for Indian tech names weakens. The broad risk-off signal is amplified by GIFT Nifty pointing to a 155-point gap-down on NSE open (~24,243).

news.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate Steady at 5.25% — No Rate Cut; Flags Iran War as Inflation Risk
news.what_happened: The Reserve Bank of India's MPC unanimously held the repo rate at 5.25% with a neutral stance. FY27 GDP projected at 6.9%, CPI at 4.6%. RBI Governor Malhotra flagged West Asia conflict and potential El Nino as upside inflation risks.
news.why_it_matters: No rate cut removes the near-term bullish trigger for banks, NBFCs, housing finance, and real estate. With US 10Y yields rising to 4.529% and Indian bond yields spiking, rate-sensitive sectors face a dual headwind. Markets had largely priced in unchanged rates, so this is status-quo but removes any positive surprise.

news.headline: GIFT Nifty at 24,254.5 Signals ~155-Point Gap-Down Open for NSE
news.what_happened: GIFT Nifty is trading at 24,254.5, down 325.5 points (-1.32%) versus Nifty futures at 24,410. The signal is driven by geopolitical crude spike, US market weakness, and Asian market sell-off (Hang Seng -0.4%, Nikkei -0.74%).
news.why_it_matters: The Nifty 50 is expected to open near 24,243, breaching key short-term support. Volatility will be elevated at open. Traders should wait for the first 15 minutes of price discovery before initiating positions. Option premiums will be rich.

picks.catalyst: WTI Crude Oil +2.76% overnight on Iran airstrikes and Strait of Hormuz shipping attacks
picks.logic: ONGC is India's largest upstream oil and gas producer. Every $1 rise in crude oil translates to approximately Rs 800-1,000 Cr per quarter in additional net realization. With WTI surging to $72.39/bbl (+2.76%) on geopolitical supply fears, ONGC's per-barrel earnings improve directly and immediately. This is a direct P&L catalyst, not just sentiment. Prev close Rs 243.9. Expect ONGC to show positive relative strength versus broad market gap-down. Wait for first 15-min stabilization before entering.

risks: Nifty gaps down ~155 pts — wait for 15-min price discovery; avoid aggressive entries at open
If Hormuz tensions escalate further, crude could spike another 2-3%, deepening India CAD and INR pressure
FII sold 2.4 lakh Nifty futures contracts on Jul 7 despite net cash buying — derivative selling pressure
US 10Y yield at 4.529% and rising — banks face MTM losses on bond portfolios if yields keep climbing
53 IPO lock-in expiries between Jul-Sep 2026 worth ~$11 billion — potential share supply overhang
Samsung/chip sector collapse (-7.6% Kospi) may extend global IT sentiment damage throughout the session
eod_summary: ONGC: +1.15% (missed target +3.0%). 0/1 picks hit target today. Nifty 50 closed at 23,882 (-2.12%).
